nodes.label: Topology Contract
nodes.summary: Canonical identity axes, ownership boundaries, impact matrix, scenario packs, and expected-vs-actual gaps.

nodes.label: Current Live Validation
nodes.summary: Live validation evidence is intentionally local-only and is not embedded in the public topology explorer.

nodes.label: channel_identity
nodes.summary: fail-closed channel/account/agent binding

nodes.label: channel_state
nodes.summary: slash-command state and active session metadata

nodes.label: channel_ingress / channel_runtime
nodes.summary: command-vs-agent-turn decision
nodes.reads: inbound platform event, identity binding, channel state
nodes.writes: runtime queue item or command outbox

nodes.label: runtime_queue / runtime_worker
nodes.summary: durable queue item lifecycle and lane concurrency
nodes.reads: pending queue, leases, runtime class config

nodes.label: media / artifact hygiene
nodes.summary: harness-contained artifact metadata, extraction summaries, prompt-safe artifact refs
nodes.reads: inbound attachments, generated media/tool receipts, artifact metadata

nodes.label: prompt / turns
nodes.summary: agent-scoped prompt bundle and selected skill context
nodes.reads: registry, prompt files, memory, channel state, artifact summaries

nodes.label: codex_runtime
nodes.summary: app-server plan, preflight, run, completion capture, tool-timeout recovery
nodes.writes: execution receipts, transcripts, completions, toolUseTimeout summaries

nodes.label: runtime_pipeline
nodes.summary: run-once orchestration, retry/dead-letter, final outbox repair
nodes.reads: queue item, Codex receipts, channel state
nodes.writes: run-once receipt, dead-letter receipt, final outbox

nodes.label: channel_delivery
nodes.summary: provider-native delivery and retry

nodes.label: progress
nodes.summary: progress narration delivery, separate from final replies

nodes.label: memory
nodes.summary: recall/lifecycle/store proposal namespaces
nodes.writes: memory receipts and graph freshness receipts

nodes.label: platform
nodes.summary: Telegram, Discord, or another channel adapter.
nodes.preservedThrough: ingress, queue, progress, outbox, delivery receipts, trace

nodes.label: accountId
nodes.summary: Provider account or bot identity when configured.

nodes.label: channelId
nodes.summary: DM/chat/channel/thread address.
nodes.preservedThrough: channel state, queue item, delivery intent, trace

nodes.label: userId
nodes.summary: Sender identity inside the channel.
nodes.preservedThrough: permission gate, channel state, session key, trace

nodes.label: agentId
nodes.summary: Runtime owner, not just persona text.
nodes.preservedThrough: provider/model defaults, Codex home, prompt files, memory namespace, runtime lane, session freshness, outbox, delivery, OperationPlan scope

nodes.label: sessionKey
nodes.summary: Conversation/session continuity key.
nodes.preservedThrough: command state, runtime queue, Codex plan, transcript, freshness guard, prompt/memory task boundary

nodes.label: runtimeClass
nodes.summary: Interactive, cron, worker, maintenance, or another runtime lane.
nodes.preservedThrough: queue selection, lease, retry/dead-letter policy, status

nodes.label: sourceQueueId
nodes.summary: Correlation for one queued turn.
nodes.preservedThrough: Codex completion, final outbox, delivery receipts, trace

nodes.label: Channel identity, allow-list, or bindings
nodes.summary: allowed vs blocked ingress; ambiguous binding fail-closed; non-main agent binding

nodes.label: Channel commands or state
nodes.summary: /new, /model, /think, /stop; same agent stale-session suppression; different agent non-suppression; fresh /new prompt/memory task boundary

nodes.label: Runtime queue or leases
nodes.summary: same-session FIFO; runtime class isolation; stale lease recovery; terminal state selection

nodes.label: Codex runtime or completion recording
nodes.summary: real final plus empty completion; already-recorded completion outbox repair; protocol error classification; active tool-use idle timeout recovery

nodes.label: Final outbox or delivery
nodes.summary: completed turn has exactly one final outbox or terminal notification; duplicate suppression by source queue/completion; final agent-reply excludes progress/narration stream content; Telegram and Discord delivery receipt trace
nodes.reviewDocs: local docs/.private/agent-harness-channel-self-check.md, docs/invariants.md, local docs/.private/release-checklist.md, this file

nodes.label: Progress delivery or narration
nodes.summary: bounded body/status edit volume; repeated text-hash suppression; terminal progress convergence; progress/narration stays out of final agent-reply; compact-before-turn fallback or fresh-thread recovery path when completion recovery is touched
nodes.reviewDocs: docs/agent-harness-assistant-narration-tech-note.md, local docs/.private/agent-harness-channel-self-check.md, docs/invariants.md, local docs/.private/release-checklist.md, this file

nodes.label: Artifact or media prompt hygiene
nodes.summary: image, audio/transcript, generated speech/media, browser capture, document/download, long tool-log/review transcript, worker-report, provider-native attachment fixtures, and Discord text/image attachments render as artifact refs plus bounded extraction summaries; raw bytes/base64/provider URLs/tool blobs are absent from main-thread prompt/continuity and Codex progress previews
nodes.reviewDocs: local .debug/round10/image-artifact-context-hygiene-tech-note-2026-06-28.md, docs/invariants.md, local docs/.private/release-checklist.md, this file

nodes.label: Prompt assembly or agent selection
nodes.summary: main and non-main prompt isolation; provider/Codex home selection; prompt file role headers; fresh /new request does not inject previous-task memory context

nodes.label: Memory or graph recall
nodes.summary: per-agent memory namespace; source-scope/filter telemetry; graph freshness receipt; no canonical writes through read-index caches

nodes.label: Supervisor or live cutover
nodes.summary: staging build, idle gate, backup/rollback receipt, post-cutover health/status/outbox checks

nodes.label: openclaw-mem-full-parity-gap
nodes.summary: openclaw-mem graph autonomous matching
nodes.designTarget: openclaw-mem / openclaw-mem-engine graph recall, capture, routeAuto/autonomous matching, provenance, and freshness operate at full product parity through the active memory owner.
nodes.currentEvidence: Graph topology extract is green for the harness support plane; memory read-path smoke is ready through explicit SQLite/vector/text/writeback migration fallback when the mem-engine bridge response is absent.
nodes.promotionGate: Bridge/sidecar emits stable recall envelopes; Qdrant/native backend selected by the memory layer; routeAuto/autonomous matching fixture passes; provenance/freshness receipts are green; fallback is not used for the proof query; canonical writes remain governed.

nodes.label: per-agent-memory-recall-compartment-gap
nodes.summary: Per-agent memory recall compartment
nodes.designTarget: Non-main and public-facing agents should receive their own memory prompt/lifecycle/canvas/writeback context by default. Legacy/global imported memory should be opt-in, trust-tagged, and visibly scoped so a public or coach agent cannot accidentally inherit private main context.
nodes.currentEvidence: Local Round10 gap-closure staging now has memory::tests::non_main_memory_prompt_context_excludes_global_imported_snapshot_by_default, which proves a non-main agent recall context uses its per-agent memory root, reports globalImportedSnapshotAllowed=false, records source scope, and filters private global imported memory. Live cutover and broader openclaw-mem full parity remain pending.
nodes.promotionGate: Before promotion, run the focused memory regression plus a status/read-path smoke that surfaces the same scope/filter telemetry for the candidate live home. Do not claim openclaw-mem full parity while bridge fallback is active or global imported recall remains a policy exception.

nodes.label: multi-agent-full-matrix-gap
nodes.summary: Multi-agent platform isolation
nodes.designTarget: Any configured agent can be added without rebuilding the pipeline, then own its workspace/prompt source, channel state, provider/model settings, memory namespace, context history, runtime lane, worker/subagent leases, and delivery receipts while sharing harness infrastructure.
nodes.currentEvidence: Imported-agent registry, per-agent session paths, per-agent /model and /think overrides, provider-specific Codex homes, per-agent memory roots, agent-aware runtime dispatch, worker master_agent_id, and subagent lifecycle receipts are implemented. The Xiaoxiaoli freshness regression showed one final-outbox guard still compared shared channel state without preserving agentId; this change fixes that guard and adds a scenario test.
nodes.promotionGate: Add or maintain a reusable matrix that creates a new configured agent and proves independent workspace/prompt files, channel command state, provider/model override, memory/context history, worker lease/subagent lifecycle, final outbox, and delivery trace while sharing the same harness queues and loops.

nodes.label: virtual-session-continuity-gap
nodes.summary: Virtual session and working-context continuity
nodes.designTarget: Long tasks should survive repeated compaction by rolling into fresh concrete sessions inside a stable virtual session, carrying a bounded working set so repeated compression does not silently distort task state. /new is a task-boundary command and must not inherit the previous virtual/task memory context.
nodes.currentEvidence: context_rollover records compact counters, virtual-session records, working-set memory, session indexes, rollover episodes, and guarded requeue receipts; prompt assembly injects working-set continuity when a continuation session has an index. Local Round10 gap-closure staging adds prompt::tests::prompt_bundle_new_command_boundary_skips_prior_task_memory_context, proving a fresh /new prompt suppresses previous-task imported memory context. Broader end-to-end rollover/final-delivery parity remains pending.
nodes.promotionGate: Add an end-to-end scenario that forces compact-threshold rollover, verifies a fresh session key under the same virtual session, checks prompt working-set injection, confirms old prepared/leased turns are not rewritten unsafely, traces final outbox/delivery back to the continuation queue item, and separately proves /new creates a new task boundary that does not inherit previous-task memory context.

nodes.label: progress-delivery-volume-gap
nodes.summary: Progress delivery and final-reply UX
nodes.designTarget: A long-running channel turn should show bounded progress updates, then converge to one final reply or terminal notification without excessive provider edits, repeated body/status churn, or non-terminal progress after completion. The body/action lane is an event-history stream and may be capped; the status/current-step lane is a heartbeat/current-summary surface and must remain editable after the body cap.
nodes.currentEvidence: A 2026-06-28 Telegram DM audit after 02:57 Taipei found one completed turn with one final outbox but more than 100 progress edit receipts, and a later in-flight turn with dozens of progress edits before any final outbox. A Discord long-running turn then showed the inverse UX failure: body/status counters reached 6 while internal events and typing indicators continued, making the visible panel look frozen. Round10 adds response.progressDeliveryMaxNonterminalUpdatesPerLane / progressDeliveryMaxNonterminalBodyUpdatesPerQueue, tracks delivered non-terminal lane counters in agent-harness.progress-delivery-state.v1, exposes volume_limited, keeps terminal convergence outside the volume cap, and adds status heartbeat after body cap via progressDeliveryStatusHeartbeatAfterBodyCapMs. progress::tests::delivery_plan_status_heartbeat_after_body_cap_is_channel_agnostic proves the same behavior for Telegram and Discord fixtures. Follow-up staging adds progress::tests::delivery_plan_status_updates_immediately_for_new_current_step_after_body_cap, proving a newly appended assistant execution summary bypasses the post-body-cap heartbeat interval and edits the status/current-step lane immediately.
nodes.promotionGate: Promote only after a replay/scenario gate proves per-queue body/action edit caps, status/current-step heartbeat after body cap, immediate current-step updates for new assistant execution summaries, repeated text-hash suppression, terminal progress convergence, final-outbox presence, and no post-terminal edit churn across Telegram and Discord.

nodes.label: progress-final-surface-gap
nodes.summary: Progress narration and final surface separation
nodes.designTarget: When assistantNarrationMode=progress_panel, progress/narration deltas should be visible only through progress panels or audit receipts. Final agent-reply outbox payloads should contain the final answer or terminal notification, not the in-progress diagnostic stream.
nodes.currentEvidence: A 2026-06-28 local Telegram DM probe found a prior long progress stream written to channel outbox as agent-reply even though the completion receipt recorded assistantNarrationMode=progress_panel, assistantFinalChars=140, and assistantNarrationItems=0. Round10 adds runtime-pipeline replay gates for already-recorded completion repair proving progress-panel narration stays out of repaired final outbox payloads for both Telegram and Discord. A later Telegram DM imagegen turn exposed the stdout-recovery subcase: a recovered completed turn had no final_answer, only progress-panel narration, and wrote that narration as final agent-reply; local follow-up staging adds codex_runtime::tests::run_codex_runtime_rejects_stdout_recovery_narration_without_final_answer, which fails closed instead of using narration/raw assistant text as final.
nodes.promotionGate: Promote only after the replay/scenario pack also covers compact-before-turn fallback or fresh-thread recovery and asserts Telegram and Discord final outbox payloads contain only the final answer while progress text stays in progress receipts/panels.

nodes.label: tool-use-timeout-recovery-gap
nodes.summary: Codex tool-use timeout recovery
nodes.designTarget: Long external tools should not be allowed to silently stall the app-server until the parent queue item is dead-lettered. The harness should stop the stalled tool path, surface the timeout to the model, and allow bounded recovery or a clear blocker report.
nodes.currentEvidence: Round10 adds active tool-use tracking for Codex item events and converts an in-flight tool idle timeout into one fresh-thread recovery prompt with toolUseTimeout receipt metadata. This is intentionally an initial guard: it stops the app-server/tool process and gives the model a bounded continuation prompt, but it is not yet a dedicated per-tool supervisor with resumable cancellation.
nodes.promotionGate: Promote to full parity only after per-tool cancellation semantics, retry budgets, trace replay, and long-review soak tests prove a timed-out tool can be killed or isolated without losing queue trace, final outbox, or follow-up model control.

nodes.label: external-review-loop-resilience-gap
nodes.summary: External review loop resilience
nodes.designTarget: External review/second-brain output is progress evidence for the parent task unless the parent queue explicitly reaches final completion. A Claude review result, timeout, or auth failure must not be mistaken for workflow closure.
nodes.currentEvidence: Local Round10 gap closure adds codex_runtime::tests::run_codex_runtime_keeps_external_review_recovery_as_evidence_not_final, which records review-only recovery output as agent-harness.external-review-evidence.v1 instead of a completion. It also adds runtime_pipeline::tests::run_runtime_queue_once_keeps_external_review_evidence_resumable_without_final_outbox, proving the parent queue remains retryable/resumable and no final outbox is written for review-only evidence.
nodes.promotionGate: Keep the runtime-pipeline replay green and add live/soak evidence before promotion: final outbox closure must only happen after the parent workflow completes or emits an explicit terminal blocker.

nodes.label: context-preflight-high-usage-gap
nodes.summary: Context preflight high-usage guard
nodes.designTarget: Bound Codex threads with very high recorded usage should compact or checkpoint before heavy image/external-tool continuations, even when prompt bytes are modest and no modelContextWindow ratio is configured.
nodes.currentEvidence: Local Round10 gap closure adds codex_runtime::tests::run_codex_runtime_preflight_compacts_high_usage_bound_thread_without_model_window, which failed first for a 136602-token replay with compactBeforeTurn=false and now passes by forcing compact through highContextUsageCompactTokenLimit.
nodes.promotionGate: Promote only after Telegram/Discord replay proves high prior bound-thread usage triggers compact/checkpoint/fresh-thread before heavy continuations, fallback does not reuse the overloaded context, and final outbox/progress surfaces remain separated.

nodes.label: generic-artifact-context-hygiene-gap
nodes.summary: Generic artifact/context hygiene
nodes.designTarget: Binary and bulky artifacts should be stored and referenced through harness artifact metadata after extraction. Durable main-session prompt, memory, continuity, progress previews, and final replies should receive bounded extracted facts plus artifact refs, not raw bytes/base64/provider URLs/document bodies/tool blobs.
nodes.currentEvidence: Round10 now extends agent-harness.inbound-media-artifact.v1 with optional lifecycleStatus and extractionSummary, adds generic prompt redaction for raw payload signatures/provider URLs/tokens/cookies, and covers Hermes-style classes with media::tests::artifact_prompt_hygiene_renders_generic_bounded_summaries_not_payloads plus runtime queue prompt coverage. Follow-up staging adds codex_runtime::tests::codex_progress_redacts_artifact_payloads_from_tool_previews, proving generated image/tool output previews do not persist raw data:image, base64, provider URLs, or token-bearing strings in progress events. Discord attachment extraction follow-up adds agent-harness-cli::tests::discord_gateway_text_attachment_becomes_bounded_artifact_metadata, agent-harness-cli::tests::discord_gateway_image_attachment_is_artifact_reference_without_payload, agent-harness-cli::tests::discord_gateway_rejects_non_discord_attachment_url_without_fetching, agent-harness-cli::tests::discord_gateway_attachment_download_failure_redacts_provider_url, agent-harness-cli::tests::discord_gateway_image_attachment_requires_supported_image_bytes, agent-harness-cli::tests::discord_gateway_json_like_attachment_uses_json_artifact_extension, and media::tests::discord_text_artifact_uri_resolves_and_passes_generic_safety, proving Discord text/image attachments are copied into artifact storage and prompt-rendered through artifact refs plus bounded text summaries without raw CDN URLs/tokens, while non-Discord URLs, raw provider-error strings, fake image bodies, and JSON-like extension gaps fail closed. This is prompt/progress/ingress-surface hygiene, not a complete artifact lifecycle service.
nodes.promotionGate: Promote only after an end-to-end artifact-heavy Telegram/Discord replay proves extraction writes bounded facts, raw artifacts remain retrievable by pointer, memory/continuity do not persist blobs, generated media/tool outputs are stored as artifacts/receipts with capped previews, and image-only compact is not used as proof for audio/browser/document/log classes.

nodes.label: image-route-vs-input-classification-gap
nodes.summary: Codex image route-vs-input classification
nodes.designTarget: Image generation receipts should distinguish a broken global image route from a specific prompt/reference input that failed to call the image tool after the route has been proven available.
nodes.currentEvidence: A Round9-4 white-lounge raw-final recheck showed known-good inputs and canary route probes succeeded through the same gateway, while the target input returned IMAGE_GENERATION_FAILED_NO_TOOL; older receipts classified that as image_tool_unavailable. Round10 makes the gateway run a route probe before returning unavailable and emits image_tool_not_called_for_input when the canary proves the route is available.
nodes.promotionGate: Keep fake-route tests plus at least one staged route canary receipt proving available route and input-specific failure classification before using the classification for production image-task fallback decisions.

nodes.label: repo-code-graph-support-gap
nodes.summary: Repo code topology graph
nodes.designTarget: A queryable repo/code knowledge graph can answer architecture, dependency, and diff-impact questions for behavior-changing changes.
nodes.currentEvidence: Topology contract exists as canonical text; openclaw-mem topology extract currently covers cron/script/repo support-plane nodes, not full Rust code relationships.
nodes.promotionGate: Understand-Anything or equivalent graph build runs with a reviewed ignore policy, public hygiene boundaries, commit hash metadata, and reviewer-confirmed usefulness against the topology contract.

nodes.label: supervisor-service-health-precedence-gap
nodes.summary: Supervisor service health precedence
nodes.designTarget: healthz, status --json, and supervisor-reconcile should present one canonical managed-service health view or explicitly label when service registry metadata is non-authoritative behind a fresher loop heartbeat.
nodes.currentEvidence: A 2026-06-28 Round10 post-cutover readback showed healthz ready=true live=true, fresh discord-gateway-loop heartbeat, and supervisor-reconcile --all --dry-run with no missing/stale services, while healthz.supervisorServices still exposed an older discord-gateway-loop service record as status=spawning / stale=true. Local follow-up staging adds status::tests::loop_status_prefers_fresh_loop_heartbeat_over_spawning_service_state and health::tests::healthz_prefers_fresh_loop_heartbeat_over_spawning_service_state, aligning status/healthz read models with reconcile precedence for a fresh alive Discord gateway heartbeat.
nodes.promotionGate: Promote only after service-state, loop-heartbeat, healthz, status --json, and reconcile output use the same precedence rules or surface stale service metadata as historical/non-authoritative, with a Discord gateway spawn-boundary scenario proving no false stale alarm when the Node heartbeat is fresh.

nodes.label: scenario-matrix-coverage-gap
nodes.summary: Release-gate scenario replay
nodes.designTarget: Every topology-sensitive change maps changed modules to required scenario packs and invariant replay.
nodes.currentEvidence: Invariants and release checklist exist; agent-boundary regression added for session freshness.
nodes.promotionGate: A reusable scenario runner or documented command pack covers agent-boundary, final-outbox, delivery, trace, and memory graph cases for changed components.

journeys.title: Channel Turn Lifecycle
journeys.summary: Follow one Discord or Telegram message from identity binding to queue, prompt, Codex runtime, final outbox, and delivery.
journeys.why: Use this when a turn times out, replies on the wrong channel, or writes progress text into the final answer.

steps.title: Prove the lane
steps.body: Confirm platform, account, channel, user, agent, and session before reconnecting prior work.

steps.title: Queue the turn
steps.body: Ingress writes a source-correlated queue item and keeps runtime class and session freshness visible.

steps.title: Assemble prompt
steps.body: Prompt files, selected skills, memory, and inbound context are scoped by the resolved agent.

steps.title: Run and recover
steps.body: Codex runtime records completion, protocol errors, tool-use timeout summaries, and fresh-thread recovery attempts.

steps.title: Deliver final
steps.body: Runtime pipeline and delivery must emit one final outbox or one terminal notification, separate from progress.

journeys.title: Multi-Agent Isolation
journeys.summary: Inspect where agentId must survive so main, Xiaoxiaoli, and future agents do not cross-suppress or cross-pollute state.
journeys.why: Use this when a non-main agent behaves like main, inherits the wrong memory, or loses final delivery because another agent owns shared state.

steps.title: Agent is a routing boundary
steps.body: agentId must survive channel state, prompt assembly, runtime lanes, outbox, delivery, and memory.

steps.title: Artifacts are scoped
steps.body: Sessions and memory artifacts can be stored under per-agent paths.

steps.title: Recall still needs stricter policy
steps.body: Xiaoxiaoli currently allows global imported fallback memory, so public/non-main agents need a recall compartment gate.

steps.title: Promotion proof
steps.body: Run the full multi-agent matrix and a memory recall regression that excludes main private/global imported memories.

journeys.title: Long Task Resilience
journeys.summary: Track the parts that keep a long running task observable without letting progress, tool use, or compaction destroy final delivery.
journeys.why: Use this after idle app-server timeouts, excessive progress edits, missing final replies, or compact/retry confusion.

steps.title: Keep the queue alive
steps.body: Leases and runtime classes prevent one slow task from hiding every other lane.

steps.title: Bound external tools
steps.body: Round10 adds an initial active tool-use idle guard and one bounded recovery prompt.

steps.title: Separate progress from final
steps.body: Progress panels can be verbose; final channel replies must stay final-answer only.

steps.title: Preserve continuity
steps.body: Virtual-session rollover must prove fresh concrete sessions without losing working context or trace.

journeys.title: Memory Ownership
journeys.summary: Understand the Store / Pack / Observe boundary, current fallback posture, and what remains before openclaw-mem parity.
journeys.why: Use this when recall quality, graph freshness, Qdrant Edge, or per-agent memory scope looks suspicious.

steps.title: Read the live posture
steps.body: The current live path is Ready through migration fallback while the mem-engine bridge response is absent.

steps.title: Keep ownership clean
steps.body: Qdrant Edge is preserved snapshot evidence and read-index/cache, not canonical write ownership.

steps.title: Fix public-agent recall
steps.body: Per-agent artifacts exist; recall must still become agent/channel-policy aware.

steps.title: Promote only with receipts
steps.body: Bridge, native backend, routeAuto, provenance, and freshness must be green before claiming parity.

journeys.title: Release Review Path
journeys.summary: Start from a changed area, jump to docs/tests, then inspect the expected-vs-actual gaps before release or cutover.
journeys.why: Use this before a behavior-changing diff, especially runtime, channel, prompt, delivery, memory, or supervisor work.

steps.title: Pick the changed area
steps.body: The impact matrix gives the minimum docs and scenario pack for a diff.

steps.title: Check open gaps
steps.body: Do not collapse usable fallback evidence into full design parity.

steps.title: Update canon first
steps.body: Topology, invariants, release checklist, and operator docs move together when expectations change.

steps.title: Regenerate the explorer
steps.body: Run the sync command after topology canon changes so the page remains a support-plane reflection.
